round0_empirical_result.summary: Round-0 proposed two terminal-corpus hypotheses (calibrated_structural_assembly via struct_size_and_token_count; calibrated_rare_value_targeting via value_distance_reached). The arbiter scored all 13 branches (harfbuzz 6, sqlite3 3, libxml2 3, curl 1) and returned rule_not_met on every one. 0 branches ever labeled across both rounds.
round0_empirical_result.interpretation: The arbiter did NOT find size_lift>=1.3 / tag_lift>=1.3 (assembly) nor value_dist_lift>1 (rare-value) on any decidable member. This empirically confirms the round-0 scheduling_caveat: a terminal seed-set snapshot does not separate calibrated from naive.

why_decidable_false.core: calibrated_energy (minimizer / CalibrationStage perf_score) is a SCHEDULING / POWER technique. Its only mechanistic difference from naive is the per-seed havoc TRIAL COUNT allocated over campaign time (energy = perf_score x visits). The mutator vocabulary is byte-identical across all 4 fuzzers (data_realities #1) and coverage-feedback admission is identical, so the advantage is a campaign-DYNAMICS property (how much energy a seed received before terminal), not a property of the terminal saved corpus.
why_decidable_false.tools_are_terminal_only: All four BUILT measurement tools read TERMINAL corpus / seed-set state: operand_enrichment (corpus byte-freq), joint_necessity (seed-set size+tokens), value_distance_reached (seed-set Hamming distance to literal), depth_reach (per-seed exec count of the branch line). None observes per-seed energy-over-time. A terminal snapshot reports WHAT the corpus contains, never HOW MUCH scheduling energy produced it.
why_decidable_false.equal_admission_kills_the_signal: Because calibrated and naive admit seeds by the SAME coverage rule with the SAME mutators, naive's corpus eventually accumulates structurally-similar and value-equally-close seeds to calibrated's -- just produced later / slower. Every signature's mechanism_summary attributes the win to 'more havoc iterations / extra mutation budget / raises the probability', i.e. faster-via-more-energy, NOT a corpus content the naive corpus cannot also reach. Therefore size_lift ~ 1.0 and value_dist_lift ~ 1.0 are the EXPECTED terminal readings even when the live scheduling advantage was real. The arbiter's all-rule_not_met result is exactly that prediction, observed.
why_decidable_false.g2_independence_is_unsatisfiable: The only terminal observable that WOULD separate calibrated from naive (calibrated's corpus reached the deep gate, naive's did not) is the resolve outcome itself -- which is the label-SOURCE, not an independent verification-SIGNAL. Verifying 'calibrated reaches deeper' with 'calibrated resolved' violates guardrail G2. There is no terminal observable independent of the resolve pattern for a pure power-schedule effect.
why_decidable_false.the_discriminating_signal_is_unbuilt_and: A real discriminator would be a per-seed energy/dynamics descriptor (compute energy_share / plateau_time over source=seed_lineage|exec_stats|coverage_timeseries) -- e.g. cumulative havoc execs per seed, or time-to-plateau per arm. No such tool exists in tool_registry.json and the registry exposes no per-seed energy accounting. Even a built energy_share tool would be confounded: a higher energy share on the calibrated side is what the scheduling technique DOES by definition, so it re-states the technique rather than independently verifying the per-branch mechanism, again straining G2.

per_member_disposition.reason: scheduling-only effect (state_or_grammar / DTD depth); terminal-corpus invisible. ALSO libxml2 has no on-disk corpus (data_realities #4), so seed_set descriptors cannot score it regardless.
scheduling-only effect (entity-ref path); terminal-corpus invisible. ALSO no on-disk libxml2 corpus.
scheduling-only probability-of-byte-flip effect (single-byte name char class); terminal-corpus invisible. ALSO no on-disk libxml2 corpus.

scheduling-only effect (preserve/extend structured TE-header seed). curl corpus exists, but round-0 arbiter returned rule_not_met (no size/tag lift) -- naive's snapshot reaches comparable structure; the advantage is energy/time, terminal-corpus invisible.
scheduling-only effect (GPOS attachment flag); rule_not_met round-0. Energy-over-time advantage not in terminal corpus.
scheduling-only effect (UNSAFE_TO_BREAK glyph flag); rule_not_met round-0.
scheduling-only effect (font-table directory -> table_index==1); rule_not_met round-0.
scheduling-only effect (variable-font cache-create branch); rule_not_met round-0.
scheduling-only effect (complete kern-table structure); rule_not_met round-0.

scheduling-only probability-of-codepoint effect (4-byte KHUDAWADI follower); rule_not_met round-0. value_distance_reached read no calibrated-vs-naive gap -- naive's seeds reach comparable distance, just slower.
scheduling-only probability-of-literal effect (literal '<' in parseable SELECT -> OP_Lt); rule_not_met round-0.
scheduling-only effect (grow long SELECT to accumulate resolver error); rule_not_met round-0.
scheduling-only effect (literal-arithmetic SELECT body -> codeInteger); rule_not_met round-0.

merges_and_collapses.round0_hypotheses_dropped: Both round-0 hypotheses (calibrated_structural_assembly, calibrated_rare_value_targeting) are dropped. They were not refuted as WRONG mechanism stories -- the signatures' attributions (more energy -> deeper structure / higher byte-flip probability) are plausible -- but they are NON-DISCRIMINABLE from naive with any BUILT terminal-corpus tool, which the arbiter's all-rule_not_met outcome confirms. Per job 2 + guardrail G3, an honest non-discriminable verdict beats a rule the data cannot support.
merges_and_collapses.no_subgroup_survives: Neither the structural-assembly subgroup (8 members) nor the rare-value subgroup (3 members) yields a discriminable terminal signal: both subgroups attribute the win to per-seed energy/trial-count, which equal-admission + identical-mutators makes invisible in the terminal corpus. No sub-shape is rescued.

notes: WHOLE-SHAPE decidable:false (honest non-discriminable). calibrated_energy is a power-SCHEDULING technique whose advantage is a campaign-DYNAMICS quantity (per-seed energy x time), NOT a property of the terminal saved corpus that every built tool reads. Equal coverage-admission + byte-identical mutators mean naive's terminal corpus reaches structurally/value-comparable seeds (just slower), so the round-0 terminal-corpus measurements (struct_size_and_token_count, value_distance_reached) read no calibrated-vs-naive lift -- the arbiter returned rule_not_met on all 13 branches across harfbuzz/sqlite3/libxml2/curl, empirically confirming the round-0 scheduling_caveat. The only would-be discriminator is a per-seed energy/plateau descriptor (energy_share / plateau_time over seed_lineage|exec_stats|coverage_timeseries) which (a) is UNBUILT and absent from tool_registry.json, and (b) would re-state the technique rather than independently verify it, straining G2. This is the spec's non-local / non-synthesizable scheduling case (CLAUDE.md benchmark-pivot: scheduling is a whole-target+campaign property, not capturable from corpus data); an honest decidable:false here is the CORRECT, paper-relevant outcome (guardrail G3), not a failure. All 13 branches route to fallback:inconclusive. NO hypothesis was invented to force labels.
